text: User: deeply review this repo -- I want you to help me with marketing, copywriting, launch strategy, specially positioning -- start with a swarm of sonnet agents to help you gather data, both from the code and from docs (no docs is to be taken too seriously -- might have hallucinations), as well as online market research -- once you've done a first exploration use a fable agent to get high-level ideas, direction, concepts. The first target is to rewrite from scratch the README.md and land high-level copywriting for the landing page, anchored in uniqueness, USP and any best copywriting practices. [...] Assistant: Good grounding. Now I'll launch a parallel research swarm — four Sonnet agents covering code-reality, docs/vision, market landscape, and audience/copy-craft — running concurrently. [...] The four research agents (code-reality, docs-vision, market-research, audience-copy) are running in the background. I'll wait for them to land before commissioning the Fable creative-direction pass, since Fable should synthesize across all four plus my doctrine read. I'll pick back up the moment they report. [...] Dense brief below. Source hierarchy: docs/product-spec/ (13 chapters, all "Status: draft, 2026-06-08" — explicitly working notes/positions-to-argue-with, NOT shipped-feature claims) is the deepest doctrine. README.md and AGENTS.md describe what's actually implemented (much narrower, verified). Wiki guides/nouns are LLM-compiled "warm/medium confidence" distillations — treat as secondary paraphrase, not source. [...] - The ENTIRE product-spec/ directory is dated 2026-06-08, marked "draft," described as "Working notes... Everything here is a draft / a position to argue with." This is the authors being honest that vision.md and agent-society.md are not commitments. [...] Now I have everything Fable needs. Commissioning the high-altitude creative-direction pass across all four briefs + the doctrine — running it synchronously so I can move straight into writing.
